item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"cease all voluntary motive activity altogether, including eating and drinking"

Motor activity I'm guessing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me just now:

"Dangit, the number 3555 is already taken" (in reference to an SCP in the works I have started, 3555 being a number I was tossing about as a placeholder)

*pause, reads*

"And by a good one! Double dangit!"

But in all seriousness, nice post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: is there in truth no beauty

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cone comes out of the top of his head and continues another 5 meters, skewed just like he is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but the second paragraph of the Description appears to serve no literary purpose and derails the pacing enough for me to downvote. If it were to disappear, I could effectively +2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ummmm, parallel or perpendicular to the beach? I meant that the part of his body (torso?) that goes over the water (at least that's how I'm reading it) should be blurrier because it's receding into the distance, not going into the water. Also the curvature of the clothing would look much different if it is positioned the way I'm interpreting it.

EDIT: Derp. Upon re-reading, I realized that it's not placed how I thought it was, but I'm even more confused now… is he standing up, and then got stretched sideways along the beach so that his body now looks like a long diagonal line? If so, I can't picture where the cone is at all.

EDIT 2: Like this? Cone where art thou

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
no part of the SCP is receding into the sea; he runs parallel to the beach, and this is just one end of it. and thank you :)

I'm planning to get back to Drake's Beach at some point, and when I do I'll get some suitable shots to manipulate in a more accurate manner. It'll be a bit of a walk until the road's open, though.

edit: i literally just took an older portrait i hadn'd posted anywhere and slanted the subject along the direction of the beach

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the image could use some work. I think the part of the SCP that's receding into the sea should be more blurry. The writing was superb though (upvoted accordingly).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: he figured out a new way to do it without any mess, tools, or fall but maybe wasn't around to see that it didn't work as he'd thought it would

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't explain why I feel this way, but somehow the idea of the guy killing himself and ending with, or killing himself via, only one of either

  • skewing
  • tearing his mind out of his body

feels incomplete to whatever part of my brain comes up with fucked-up ideas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the body horror here, nicely original and explained well. I'm not sure the "cone of overlapping brain functions" part adds much beyond that; if nothing else, it's pretty obvious what's going on. Still, +1 for originality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Section of SCP-3555. Click to enlarge.

I'm not falling for this one again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While this doesn't pack the emotional punch that SCP-2784 has, it's so uniquely weird that I can't not love it. I'll always be a sucker for creative, well-written SCProcedures, so that's a big plus too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is gloriously weird and feels complete without ever really showing its hand. A masterful work. There are a few small things that I would have phrased differently but I'll chalk that up to writing style more than any error on the author's part. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my favorite authors writing something just utterly bizzare yet sad? I'm sold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On a related note… https://www.nps.gov/pore/planyourvisit/roadclosures.htm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: though i love those car boys, i am willing to sign a notarized statement to the effect of "I had the idea for a skewed man by the sea before nick and griffin godtrashed anything"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NICK NICK NICK NICK NICK NICK NICK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking ace, this is why I continue reading this site for.

Here is an article. I will be back in three years to write SCP-4555.

Image attribution: all-original, baby.

Discussion questions:

  • What form does the corpse of a dead mind take?
  • How can we get people suffering from mental illness the help they need?
  • Was he successful?
